mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with t-stat > 2.0

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that immediately suppress S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE crosses 4.0 threshold but 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of cases

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of observations

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing contraction, which reduces equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines but 20-day forward returns are positive with statistical significance (p < 0.05) during late-cycle periods

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 295.0, persistent inflation expectations force aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL remains above 295.0 but 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 100,000 and stable unemployment below 4.0 percent confirms labor market resilience, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint condition of PAYEMS growth > 100k and UNRATE < 4.0 yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,500,000 units during a housing market correction indicate severe consumer balance sheet stress, which transmits to broader equity markets and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST stays below 1500 but 20-day forward returns are positive with t-stat > 1.96 during housing correction periods

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect improving manufacturing activity and supply chain normalization, which directly boosts S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 5-day returns in > 55% of observations across all regimes
